title: Resampling
summary: Record your own mix output back into Ableton as an audio track. Used to capture a mix at a certain stage, create creative samples from your own sounds, or bounce CPU-heavy parts to audio.
steps: Set the track's input to 'Resampling' from the Input Type dropdown (below the track name in Session view)
Arm the track for recording (click the arm button on the track)
Press Play and Record — the track records whatever is playing through Ableton's Master output
Stop recording. The recorded audio clip contains exactly what the Master output produced during that period
Drag the recorded clip back into your project as an audio sample, or use it as a creative starting point
tips: Resampling is a fast alternative to exporting: no need to open the export dialog. Just record the Master output to a track
Creative resampling: play a complex chord pad through a reverb, record the result. Now you have an audio recording of that sound you can slice, pitch-shift, and process further

Resample before you freeze: if you want to capture a moment in a build (e.g. the last 4 bars before the drop with all effects and automation), resample it — you have an audio recording of that exact state
The resampled audio includes all effects processing — unlike Freeze (which preserves the track separately). Resampling commits the full chain output to audio
practice_hint: In any rep, add a new audio track, set its input to 'Resampling', arm it, and record 4 bars of your loop. Listen to the resampled clip — it should sound identical to your mix. This is your mix captured as audio.
ableton_version_note: Available in all editions of Live 9+.
